basis: Existing production GHL sub-account (Ghstz8eIsHWLeXek47dk) is the starting point: keep the parts that work, fix how deal data is stored on opportunities, and add the missing pieces from the original Target plan (including the nurture sequences). Cody's builds are for reference only. One GHL sub-account with a separate calendar for each clinic. Lobbie confirmed this works with their EMR: one GHL account can connect to multiple Lobbie clinics as long as each clinic has its own calendar, and we already do.
principles: One workflow per job, with clinic as a variable (opportunity.location + a Locations custom object). Never clone a workflow just to change the clinic name.
One sales pipeline (Lead to Close). Retention & Renewals, Referrals, and Instagram DM are kept separate because they play different roles — not because the clinic is different.
Facts about the sale go on the Opportunity. Facts about the member (identity, consent, current membership) go on the Contact.
First-party data collection on our own sites; every send to Meta and Google goes out server-side from our SAC app. No third-party pixels on pages that discuss health.
Every custom field has exactly one writer. Every ad-platform conversion is de-duplicated (each event has an event_id and an outcome_processed_at timestamp).
Build changes in production as drafts. Going live means flipping a draft to published — not migrating between accounts.

limitation_rules.rule: An opportunity field can only be read or written when the workflow knows WHICH deal it's working on
limitation_rules.response: Every opportunity write happens right after a step that creates, updates, or finds the deal (or the workflow is triggered by an opportunity event). A stamp step never runs on a bare contact trigger.

limitation_rules.rule: You cannot trigger a workflow from a specific opportunity field changing (e.g. "when sale_outcome is set")
limitation_rules.response: All triggers key off contact events (form submitted, tag added, appointment). Opportunity fields are written as OUTPUTS inside those flows. renewal_date deliberately lives on the Contact so we can use GHL's native date-based trigger.

limitation_rules.rule: Workflows cannot write dollar amounts to opportunity monetary fields (GHL limitation on merge tags for numeric fields)
limitation_rules.response: The PCC form is the only writer for every dollar field. GHL's built-in Opportunity Value carries net revenue for reporting.

limitation_rules.rule: Dropdown writes must exactly match one of the option values
limitation_rules.response: Dropdowns store short codes (sold / nosale / mut / mar, new / renewal, etc.). Workflow writes use literal codes per branch, never copied from another field with different options.

limitation_rules.rule: Contact fields have superpowers opportunity fields do not: form writes, specific field-change triggers, and native date triggers
limitation_rules.response: Anything that needs those triggers or that a member fills in as INPUT stays on the Contact. Per-deal OUTPUTS live on the Opportunity.

limitation_rules.rule: Reading a field right after writing it in the same workflow can race the save
limitation_rules.response: Insert a 30–60 second wait between any field write and a downstream branch or trigger that reads it.

limitation_rules.rule: Every PUT to the workflow endpoint rewrites the entire step file from the request payload
limitation_rules.response: Every programmatic save includes the full templates array with the diff lists (created/modified/deleted step ids) and the version number. Take a local snapshot before every write.

opportunity_final.writer: WF-05 workflow stamp in R1; PCC form in R3
opportunity_final.status: Fix in R2 alignment window: prod currently lets multiple values be selected. Recreate as single-select with codes. The field id will change, so every workflow condition and form reference must update in the same block.
Fix in R2 alignment window: currently uses labels New/Renewal, flip to codes.
Exists with correct codes. Deduplicate: production has a second field named appointment_status. Keep appt_status, retire the duplicate.

Consolidate in R2: production has THREE PCC fields (patient_care_consultant with first names, patient_care_consultant_id and patient_care_consultant_pcc with full names, all with different rosters). Keep one, retire the other two, migrate the data.
Already exists with the full clinician roster.
Fix in R2 alignment window: flip label options to codes. Product 2/3, term 2/3, and price 1/2/3 are deprecating — line-item detail beyond the primary product moves to SAC in Supabase.

opportunity_final.writer: PCC form (only writer allowed by GHL)
opportunity_final.status: Exists — this is the gross program amount. GHL's built-in Opportunity Value carries net collected.

opportunity_final.writer: PCC form (only writer allowed by GHL)

opportunity_final.status: Fix in R2 alignment window: currently uppercase, flip to lowercase codes.

Deprecate on the Opportunity: the Contact owns the current renewal_date (Contact date-triggers drive renewal campaigns). Move to zz Deprecated in R2.

form_tweak_r1.summary: R1 is ADDITIVE-ONLY on the existing PCC Sales Form: add missing inputs, change nothing PCCs already use. Option-value flips to codes are behavior-touching (live workflows branch on today's prose values) and execute in the R3 alignment window together with the workflow condition updates.

monetary fields are form-only writers (GHL limitation)

workflow_registry_notes.note: Lead Capture and Attribution: stamps frozen attribution + location on the opportunity at create; emits lead_created to SAC via webhook step
Booking Confirmation and Reminders: booking events come server-side from the booking app, not from this workflow; cancel exit built in
Clinic Outcome Router: gates on appt_status BEFORE reading sale_outcome; stamps outcome/PCC/provider/term/referrer + outcome_processed_at; emits appt_outcome to SAC
Post-Visit Won and Onboarding: sets contact.renewal_date and membership_status; emits opportunity_won (new|renewal) to SAC with value
Post-Visit A&D Nurture: branches on opportunity.nosale_reason codes (cost/fear/partner/timing/decision) per the locked taxonomy
Long-Term Nurture + renewal campaigns keyed off contact.renewal_date custom-date trigger
Call Disposition Handler: inbound calls become contacts with lead_source=phone; emits call_lead to SAC (closes the Google call-conversion blind spot)
REDEFINED: Ad-Platform Conversions no longer sends to Meta/Google directly. It is the SAC webhook emitter set; SAC owns all platform sends, dedupe, and retries

sac.name: SAC — Server-side Attribution & Conversions
sac.stack: Supabase is the single database vendor. SAC uses two Supabase projects: an Attribution project (anonymized event store) and an Operations project (Force's clinical grid data). Serverless workers run on Vercel.
sac.collection: Thin first-party JavaScript on menswellnesscenters.com and book.menswellnesscenters.com captures click IDs (gclid, wbraid, gbraid, fbclid, msclkid), UTM parameters, Facebook browser identifiers (fbp/fbc), and consent state, and writes them into hidden GHL contact fields plus localStorage on page load. NO third-party ad-platform pixels load on pages that discuss health conditions. All actual sends to Meta and Google happen from SAC on the server.
sac.identity: Join keys are GHL contact_id, opportunity_id, and appointment_id (opaque, non-PHI ids). Click IDs are the fallback join. No email or phone is stored in clear text — if the HIPAA posture allows identifiers on an event, they are hashed (SHA-256) at ingest time; raw values are never stored.
sac.storage: Two logical Supabase projects. Attribution project tables: events (append-only, unique event_id), conversions_outbox (queued sends with status), reconciliation_daily. Operations project tables: appointments, dispositions, sync_events, plus Supabase Auth for Force. All tables have RLS enabled. Retention window default 25 months (matches ad-platform lookback plus audit needs), minimum-necessary access roles per table, BAA tier verification is an R1 gate.
sac.junk_filter: Events are only written when a real server-side object is created (contact created via the booking API, appointment booked via the booking API, opportunity won via a GHL workflow webhook). This filters out the pixel-only page-view events that previously created 965 consent-page ghost contacts — those never reach the outbox.

event_contract.source: Booking app or WordPress form (server-side) or WF-01 webhook
event_contract.sends: Meta Lead (proxy value $230), Google Ads secondary lead conversion
event_contract.notes: Only fires after submitLead() returns a real contact. Pixel-only page views do not qualify.

event_contract.source: Booking app server, after bookAppointment() resolves
event_contract.sends: Meta Schedule (proxy value $800), Google Ads primary booked_appointment conversion
event_contract.notes: Reuses the same GTM contract event name so the existing tags still work. The /confirmed page load never fires this.

event_contract.source: RingCentral or GHL inbound call event, via WF-12
event_contract.sends: Google call conversions, reconciled to CRM contacts
event_contract.notes: Stamps lead_source=phone. Closes the historical Google call-conversion blind spot.

event_contract.sends: Google offline conversion won_new (real dollar value), Meta MembershipWon custom event
event_contract.notes: Trains acquisition bidding. Must be separate from renewals.

event_contract.sends: Google offline conversion won_renewal (separate action), Meta membership renewal (separate event)
event_contract.notes: Kept out of the acquisition bidding signal so Smart Bidding doesn't optimize toward existing members.

event_contract.source: RESERVED for the future Lobbie EMR integration
event_contract.sends: Internal only until the integration is validated
event_contract.notes: The ingest endpoint is defined now so the integration can turn on without a code change after Lobbie go-live.

activation.google: Offline Conversion Import + Enhanced Conversions for Leads: gclid/wbraid + hashed email/phone (consent-gated), conversion actions: booked_appointment (primary), won_new (primary, real value), won_renewal (secondary), lead (secondary diagnostic). 90-day click lookback backfill at cutover.
activation.meta: Conversions API server events: Lead, Schedule, MembershipWon custom event; event_id dedupe against any remaining browser events; hashed identifiers only when consented; generic event_source_url (no condition/service params); test_event_code validation before live.
activation.values: Proxies until Won flows: $800 booking_complete, $230 lead. Real Opportunity Value at won_new/won_renewal.

hipaa_decision.question: What personally identifiable data may leave for Meta and Google, and on which events?
hipaa_decision.option_a: CONSENTED IDENTIFIERS: capture express marketing consent at the funnel form (before the visit). Send hashed email/phone on lead_created and booking_complete events. Won events reuse the SAME identifiers already captured before the visit plus the click ID — no new clinical-context data is added.
hipaa_decision.option_b: VALUE-ONLY: on every event that happens after booking, send only the click ID, the conversion value, and the event time. Never send user identifiers after a person becomes a member. Match quality on offline imports is lower, but identity risk is near zero.
hipaa_decision.recommendation: HYBRID: use Option A for pre-visit funnel events (lead_created, booking_complete) where consent is captured at the form. Use Option B posture for Won events. Document this as the standing decision and revisit with counsel before ever enabling Google Enhanced Conversions on Won.
hipaa_decision.hard_rules: No condition, service, or medication data appears in any URL or event payload. All GHL ids referenced in payloads are opaque. Consent state is stored per contact and enforced at the outbox, not at the collection step.

sac.measurement: Daily reconciliation dashboard in mwc-admin compares platform-reported conversions vs the CRM truth set (real contacts, real booked appointments, real won opportunities). Signal-drop alerts fire when outbox failures or daily deltas exceed thresholds. Monthly truth-set review is built from opportunities, not tags.
sac.delivery: Outbox pattern: sends are queued, retried with exponential backoff, moved to a dead-letter queue after N failures, and can be replayed on demand. Credentials (Meta system-user token, Google developer token) live in a secret manager with a documented rotation runbook.
sac.consolidation_2026_07_19: Neon retired. Force migrates to Supabase Operations project (MWC-Pulse). Steps: point writers at Supabase, diff/reconcile most recent rows, cut auth over to Supabase Auth, delete the Neon project.

transition.detail: Pause/demote GADS-Qualify, GADS-Schedule page-load tags and premature mwc_lead; primary = event-based booking only

transition.step: 2. Ship R1 fields + form tweak + stamps in prod (drafts)
transition.detail: Field model created/aligned, WF-05/06 stamp pattern, WF-01 attribution stamp
transition.gate: test contact per outcome code, diff-harness zero blockers

transition.step: 3. SAC ingest live in shadow mode
transition.detail: Events flow to Supabase, no platform sends; reconcile 1 week against CRM truth
Meta test_event_code + Google diagnostics; dedupe verified against any remaining browser events
Secondary -> validate 2 weeks -> promote to primary -> demote old; learning-phase managed deliberately
Trailing 90-day won conversions uploaded with real values
Old CAPI/Sheets workflows off, GTM thank-you tags off, parked contact fields retired
transition.gate: reconciliation stable 30 days + rollback plan on file

What's left is CLEANUP (aligning dropdown option values to codes, removing duplicate fields), not new field creation.

alignment_window.summary: The changes below alter live workflow behavior and MUST land together in one maintenance window (R2), at the same time as the corresponding PCC form option changes and the workflow condition updates. Landing any one of them alone would break routing for the next submission. All ADDITIVE work (new fields, webhook steps on drafts, SAC ingest) already shipped in R1 without any behavior change.
items: Recreate sale_outcome as a single-select dropdown with codes. The field id changes, so every workflow condition and form reference updates in the same block.
Change the option values to codes on: sale_type, pay_type, product_sold_1, nosale_reason (locked five-objection list), medical_intake_status.
Consolidate the three PCC fields down to one field, one canonical PCC roster. Migrate data from the retired fields, update the form and the bonus workflow.
Delete the duplicate appointment_status field; keep appt_status.
Recreate outcome_processed_at as a Text field storing an ISO timestamp (currently a Date field, which only stores days).
Park deprecated opportunity fields in a zz Deprecated folder (nothing is deleted).

target_release_findings.summary: Audit of the -Target Release draft workflows in production (WF-01..16, built 2026-07-11). Result: 15 of 16 workflows have no trigger set — mechanical to fix and now DONE (68 triggers ported 2026-07-19). The early-funnel cadence in the drafts was too slow versus the live workflows the drafts are replacing — the cadence standard below adopts the proven live timing. Two workflows are large enough to split into a router plus child sequences; six workflows are skeletal and need their designed mechanics ported in.
target_release_findings.triggers: Fixed: 68 triggers ported from the live workflows they replace, filters copied verbatim, all workflows still draft. Note: the GHL API ignores the inactive flag when creating triggers, so triggers show as 'Active' in the UI even on draft workflows. Drafts never fire regardless, so this is safe — but per-trigger active/filter review is a formal step of the publish gate.

cadence_standard.principle: Adopt the PROVEN live cadence for early funnel; day-scale only after the first 48 hours. Quiet hours 8am-9pm contact time on every step; first SMS of each sequence carries STOP.
cadence_standard.speed_to_lead: First SMS within 3 minutes of lead creation (live 02. NON BOOKED NEWLEADS proves 3-min first touch). Draft WF-01 (2-3 min) is fine; keep.
cadence_standard.non_booked_recovery: Live proven burst: +3m, +1m, +5m, +15m in the first half hour, then 24h and 36h. Draft WF-02 waits 2 HOURS between early touches - too slow; retime to the live burst then 20h/1d/2d tail.
cadence_standard.booking_confirmation: Draft WF-03 has 5 SMS but only one 1-hour wait and no appointment-relative anchors. Reminders must anchor to appointment time (T-1d, T-2h) via appointment-based trigger/wait, like live 03b (immediate confirm ask, 15m/1-2h re-asks).
cadence_standard.no_show_recovery: Draft WF-08 (15m, 45m, 23h, 2d, 2d, 2d) is directionally right; live adds a long tail (7d, 7d, 30d, 180d) via Nurture: Missed - keep the tail in WF-09 handoff.
cadence_standard.nurture: Day-scale cadence in WF-07/09 (1-5 day gaps) is correct for post-visit sequences; no change.

length_and_context: TOO LONG (split): WF-07 A&D Nurture is 97 nodes / 48 waits; WF-09 Long-Term is 61 nodes / 30 waits. Split into a thin router + per-objection (cost/fear/partner/timing/decision) or per-phase child sequences via add-to-workflow. Editing a 97-node graph re-versions the whole file on every save - operationally risky.

TOO THIN (finish): WF-10 Feedback (1 node), WF-11 Compliance (2), WF-13 Conversions (2), WF-12 Call Disposition (3), WF-15 Referral (4), WF-16 Comms Edge (5) are skeletons missing their designed mechanics (survey branch, DND authority, SAC webhook emitters, call handling, IVR/missed-call edge).
